jobTitle: PhD Student (f/m/d) First-Principles Calculations of the Electronic Properties of Single Metal Atom Chains
jobDescription: With cutting-edge research in the fields of ENERGY, HEALTH, and MATTER, around 1,500 employees from more than 70 nations at Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR) are committed to mastering the great challenges facing society today. The Institute for Ion Beam Physics and Materials Research is dedicated to the study of materials and their nanostructures for future applications in information technology based on electrical, magnetic, or optical functionalities. The Group 'Atomistic simulations of irradiation-induced phenomena' is looking for a PhD Student (f/m/d) to carry out first-principles calculations within the framework of the density-functional theory to understand the atomic structure and electronic properties of chains of metal atoms embedded into 2D transition metal dichalcogenides. This is a part-time position (29.25 h/week) with a contract term until 29 February 2028, starting on 1 March 2025.
jobTasks: Carry out first-principles calculations within the framework of the density-functional theory aimed at understanding the atomic structure and electronic properties of chains of metal atoms embedded into 2D transition metal dichalcogenides
Model the synthesis of such structures
Develop dedicate software for modelling electronic transport in the network of connected chains
Closely collaborate with the experimental groups involved in the project
